summary: Prepared a scoped repair for canonical PR #93869 on `clownfish/repair-51868-qr-rendering`. The patch keeps normal code-block `data-code` payloads raw, marks only QR/block-art payloads with `data-code-encoding="block-art-json"`, decodes only marked payloads before clipboard copy, preserves legacy raw payload behavior, and adds focused coverage for normal, truncated, and encoded-looking raw copy cases. No GitHub comments, labels, closures, merges, PR creation, or pushes were performed.

repair_candidate.summary: Repair #93869 so only QR/block-art copy payloads are encoded and explicitly marked, while ordinary code-block payloads and existing raw `data-code` values remain raw. Decode only marked block-art payloads in the chat copy handler so leading quiet-zone spaces survive DOM parsing without mis-decoding normal code snippets.
repair_candidate.pr_title: UI: render half-block QR output in web chat
repair_candidate.pr_body:
## Summary
- Repairs the existing #93869 branch so QR/block-art copy payloads preserve leading quiet-zone spaces after DOM parsing.
- Keeps ordinary code-block `data-code` payloads raw and marks only block-art payloads with `data-code-encoding="block-art-json"`.
- Decodes only marked block-art payloads in the chat copy handler, while retaining support for existing raw payloads.
- Adds focused coverage for normal block-art copy, truncated block-art copy, normal raw code copy, and encoded-looking raw payloads.

## Credit
This continues to carry forward the original fix idea and reproduction from @emg110 in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/51868. Thanks @emg110 for the source PR and proof.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/markdown.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 ui/src/ui/markdown.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts ui/src/ui/markdown.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- Codex autoreview: clean, no accepted/actionable findings

## Follow-up gate
Run `pnpm check:changed` after applying/pushing in a normal checkout; this worker checkout had shallow-branch merge-base limitations that made changed-lane detection over-broad.
